config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diutamakan tanpa kontrol, mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap minoritas atau orang yang tidak berdaya. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan dasar seseorang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan kuat terhadap hak tiap individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically override the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I have seen how strong social priorities can help with order, development, and collective action, and I do value that, especially when a society needs stability or faces major challenges. But I also think it is dangerous when “society” is used as a blanket excuse to silence individuals, because once personal rights become too weak, ordinary people lose protection against abuse. A good society should balance both: collective interests should guide policy, but basic individual rights must remain a hard limit.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju jika kepentingan masyarakat ditempatkan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, membungkam kritik, atau mengorbankan kelompok yang sebenarnya tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, apalagi saat bencana atau krisis, tetapi itu harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dilanggar seenaknya. Buat saya, masyarakat yang adil bukan yang paling patuh pada kolektif, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu tanpa kehilangan rasa tanggung jawab bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective in China, I understand why people value collective goals, social stability, and coordinated action, because those can produce real public benefits, especially in times of crisis or rapid development. But I also think the phrase "for the good of society" is too easily used to justify suppressing criticism or ignoring ordinary people’s rights, and once that happens, the people with less power pay the price. A better principle is that society can set common goals, but individual rights should remain the limit that collective power cannot cross.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus berada di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering bisa berubah jadi alat untuk menekan kritik, mengabaikan minoritas, atau membenarkan tindakan sewenang-wenang dari pihak yang lebih kuat. Kepentingan kolektif memang penting, terutama saat krisis, tetapi justru karena itu hak dasar harus jadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar begitu saja. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling keras menuntut kepatuhan, melainkan yang mampu menjaga keseimbangan antara tanggung jawab sosial dan perlindungan terhadap individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be treated as a blank check to override individual rights. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of collective priorities like stability, development, and social coordination, and I do not dismiss them at all, but I have also seen how easily “the public good” can become a convenient excuse for silencing criticism or protecting the powerful. When that happens, ordinary people lose the very protections that make society trustworthy in the first place. So I would say the real test of a good society is whether it can pursue common goals without crossing the hard line of basic individual rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
